mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue growth, so higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if walk-forward tests show RSAFS 3-month growth has Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows or triggered positive retail-sales momentum does not outperform baseline 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment falls from an elevated level, recession risk and household income uncertainty decline, causing equity risk premia to compress and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months while UNRATE > 8%
failure_condition: Reject if after this trigger the S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in less than 55% of cases or mean triggered 60-day returns are not above unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned below 0.5% and industrial production is rising, liquidity remains abundant while real activity improves, so discount-rate support and earnings upgrades jointly drive positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce higher 20-day returns than either FEDFUNDS < 0.5% alone or INDPRO growth alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening factory output and earnings revisions, so falling INDPRO momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines in two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or average triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels signals improving credit conditions, construction activity, and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average rises by more than 5% while HOUST level is below 700
regime_hint: housing-led recovery from depressed construction levels
failure_condition: Reject if the low-level housing rebound trigger has a 60-day return hit rate below 55% or its average return is not statistically above baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: If CPI accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates fall but inflation-risk and future-tightening fears dominate, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration under FEDFUNDS < 1% is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if the interaction term is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects broad demand recovery and supports aggregate earnings expectations; higher GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 vintage at the decision date was 2010-07-01 value 13278.5
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth has Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive 5-year walk-forward windows or does not improve 60-day return classification versus the unconditional positive rate.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains accompanied by a non-rising unemployment rate signal genuine labor-market improvement rather than labor-force exit noise, supporting consumption and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month net change > 300 thousand and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-and-unemployment trigger fails to beat the unconditional 5-day positive return rate or if PAYEMS gains with rising UNRATE perform equally well.
